headline: Why is Donald Trump so mean?
description: By shamelessly flouting democratic norms and flaunting his amorality, brutality and bad faith, the American president restores pride to his humiliated electoral base, unsettles his opponents, and subverts the rules of the political game.

headline: Israel renews Lebanon strikes, forcing Syria border crossing to close
Trump appears to extend Iran deadline in cryptic post
Key facts to understand cadmium, an emerging public health issue
description: Cadmium, a heavy metal toxic to human health, is found in many everyday foods. Children and women are most affected by this carcinogenic contaminant, which is the subject of growing concern.

headline: Sociologist Dominique Méda on the 'lessons of Lionel Jospin and Jürgen Habermas'
description: As the race for the 2027 French presidential election gets underway, the former prime minister, a champion of solemnity in politics, and the German philosopher, a leading thinker on democratic Europe, should serve as sources of inspiration, writes the researcher in her column.

headline: Sex is an art form hidden in the shadows
description: Moral panic over the pleasures of the flesh prevents us from recognizing the artistic dimension of sexuality, as well as from passing down the best practices, argues Le Monde columnist  Maïa Mazaurette.

headline: Will Self: 'Our historical condition is a peculiar mixture of omniscience and impotence'
description: We are bombarded by a swarm of signals too numerous to interpret in time, and the world appears both predetermined and uncontrollable, observes the British writer in an op-ed for Le Monde.

headline: Pope Leo urges 'those who have the power to unleash wars' to 'choose peace'
Artemis II astronauts begin fifth day on historic Moon mission
'The power struggle between Saudi Arabia and the UAE is only on hold'

Looking for Cécile, a 'bad girl' in France's sinister former 'preservation schools'
US and Israel increasingly target Iran's civilian infrastructure

Willo Perron, the influential designer who stages Rihanna's and Chanel's shows
'Retirees are no happier than working people'

China's execution of a Frenchman convicted of drug trafficking casts a chill on relations with Paris
Le Duc brings the fresh taste of the sea to Paris
description: For nearly 60 years, Le Duc, a family-run Parisian institution known for its delicate fish recipes, has drawn a loyal following who come to savor impossibly thinly sliced, pearly raw sea bass or langoustines with red shiso and wasabi.

headline: Photographer Francesca Allen celebrates the power of long hair in Lithuania
description: In 2024, British photographer Francesca Allen traveled to Kaunas, Lithuania, to attend an annual competition honoring the country's longest hair, a symbol of female power. This age-old tradition is carried on by girls and women of all ages. A beautiful book, co-published by Steidl and the fashion house Chloé, pays tribute to her work.

headline: Pre-election Hungary's mood is one of twilight for Orban's rule
description: After 16 years of unchallenged rule, Hungary's ultra-conservative prime minister is, for the first time, seeing his supremacy challenged. With days until the April 12 legislative elections, pro-European challenger Peter Magyar is leading in the polls. In the ruling party's eastern strongholds, huge Chinese battery factories have sparked public backlash.

headline: 'Donald Trump is unwittingly, but significantly, advancing the energy transition'
The EU must respond to the challenges posed by Viktor Orban
Death penalty for Palestinians accused of 'terrorist' murder continues Israeli government's downward spiral
